test_a_nonlinear.description: t-SNE and UMAP on identity latents — can nonlinear methods find clusters PCA missed?
test_a_nonlinear.verdict: NO — all scores negative, worse than PCA

test_b_scene_controlled.description: Same prompt template × different seeds — does controlling for scene reveal identity clusters?
test_b_scene_controlled.verdict: MARGINAL improvement but still negative. Ch3 20d = -0.071 vs Exp7's -0.084

test_c_clip.description: CLIP text embeddings — do celebrity names cluster in conditioning space?
test_c_clip.verdict: YES — strong positive clustering! Identity is linearly separable in CLIP space

key_insight: Identity information is linearly separable in CLIP text embedding space (silhouette +0.40) but NOT in the VAE latent space (all negative). This means identity lives upstream in the conditioning, not downstream in the pixel/latent representation. The UNet distributes identity across all channels during denoising, making it inseparable in the output latent.
